entities.name: RAINWATER
entities.description: Water falling as rain on the land, the primary resource for water conservation

entities.name: IN SITU WATER CONSERVATION
entities.description: The practice of conserving water where it falls on the land to improve agricultural productivity

entities.name: FARMING SYSTEMS
entities.description: The integrated components of farming, ranging from land preparation to crop harvest

entities.name: PLOUGHING
entities.description: The process of preparing the land for planting as part of the farming system

entities.name: HARVEST
entities.description: The process of gathering mature crops as the final stage of the farming system

entities.name: CROP
entities.description: The agricultural plants grown within the farming system

entities.name: DEEP VERTISOLS
entities.description: A type of deep, clay-rich soil that can show productivity increases with water conservation

entities.name: MEDIUM RAINFALL
entities.description: An intermediate level of precipitation that influences the effectiveness of water conservation

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The measure of agricultural output, which can increase three to five-fold with proper water management

entities.name: LAND DEVELOPMENT
entities.description: The process of improving land to enhance water utilization, often executed on a watershed basis

entities.name: WATER UTILISATION
entities.description: The effective use of water resources on agricultural land

entities.name: WATERSHEDS
entities.description: Geographic land areas that channel rainfall to a common outlet, used as a basis for land development

entities.name: RESOURCE MANAGEMENT SYSTEMS
entities.description: Systems designed for the improved management of agricultural resources

entities.name: ORGANIZATIONAL AND ADMINISTRATIVE STRUCTURES
entities.description: The organizational frameworks required for the operation and maintenance of resource management systems
